info.title: Rumil API
info.description: Read-only browsing API for the Rumil research workspace, plus the /api/jobs endpoints used by the job-monitoring UI: POST /api/jobs/orchestrator-runs submits a Kubernetes Job and GET /api/jobs lists recent orchestrator Jobs in the cluster.

get.description:
List orchestrator Jobs (newest first), capped at 200 rows.

Filtered to `rumil.ink/run-kind=orchestrator` so non-orchestrator Jobs
in the namespace are excluded. Jobs that pre-date the run-id/workspace/
question metadata rollout — or any other Job that happens to share the
run-kind label without our full metadata — are silently skipped.

schema.title: Response List Jobs Api Jobs Get

get.description:
List essays at the current essay-cache schema version.

Old cached snapshots (pre-rename, ``schema_version < SCHEMA_VERSION``)
are skipped by default — they often have stale prefix_config_hashes
and would show up as a near-duplicate of the freshly-cached entry,
confusing the inspect dropdown. Pass ``include_legacy=true`` to
include them anyway (no UI surfaces this yet; it's a debug escape).

schema.title: Response List Essays Api Versus Essays Get
Response Get Essay Sources Api Versus Essays  Essay Id  Sources Get

get.description:
Chronological (recent-first) feed of versus texts and judgments.

Independent of the aggregator pipeline on /results — this view is
about "what just ran", so it returns rows regardless of staleness or
contamination, with flags so the FE can mark them visually. Each
table is independently capped at ``limit``.

Works without versus config (staleness flags are skipped if config
is missing — useful when poking at the table from a fresh checkout).

Look up the verbatim row for a single judgment key.

Used by the side-panel inspector on /versus/results so a reader can see
the prompt + reasoning + raw response that produced a verdict. The key
is the row's primary-key UUID, so this is a direct lookup — no scan.

Post-hoc bias / n-floor / per-essay sanity over the judgments log.

Filters mirror /results so the pane's numbers line up with the
matrix the operator is currently looking at.

schema.title: Response Fire Api Exchange Forks Post
Response List Forks Api Exchange Forks Get
Response Delete Fork Api Exchange Forks  Fork Id  Delete
Response List Moves Api Atlas Registry Moves Get
Response List Dispatches Api Atlas Registry Dispatches Get
Response List Call Types Api Atlas Registry Calls Get
Response List Page Types Api Atlas Registry Pages Get
Response List Prompts Api Atlas Registry Prompts Get
Response List Workflows Api Atlas Workflows Get
Response List Workflow Runs Endpoint Api Atlas Workflows  Name  Runs Get
Response List Projects Api Projects Get
Response List Project Runs Api Projects  Project Id  Runs Get
Response Get Links From Api Pages  Page Id  Links From Get
Response Get Links To Api Pages  Page Id  Links To Get

get.description: Pages that depend on this page (inbound DEPENDS_ON links).

schema.title: Response Get Dependents Api Pages  Page Id  Dependents Get

get.description: Pages that this page depends on (outbound DEPENDS_ON links).

schema.title: Response Get Dependencies Api Pages  Page Id  Dependencies Get

get.description:
Aggregate stats over all pages/links/calls in a project.

Baseline rows are always included; when `staged_run_id` is provided as a
query param, rows from that staged run are also included and its mutation
events (supersede_page, delete_link) are overlayed.

Aggregate stats over the 2-hop undirected neighborhood around a question.

Returns 404 if the target page is not a question. Staged-run visibility
matches get_project_stats.

schema.title: Response List Root Questions Api Projects  Project Id  Questions Get
Response List Calls Api Projects  Project Id  Calls Get
Response Get Child Calls Api Calls  Call Id  Children Get
Response Get Call Events Api Calls  Call Id  Events Get
Response List Experiments Api Experiments Get
Response List Llm Exchanges Api Calls  Call Id  Llm Exchanges Get
Response Get Page Run Api Pages  Page Id  Run Get

BaseExchangeOut.title: BaseExchangeOut
BaseExchangeOut.description: Reconstructed base exchange — what the fork editor pre-populates.

CallSummary.title: CallSummary
CallSummary.description:
Lightweight Call representation for tree views — excludes bulky fields
like review_json, result_summary, and context_page_ids.

CallTypeStats.title: CallTypeStats
CallTypeStats.description: Empirical stats for one CallType across recent runs.

Cell.title: Cell
Cell.description:
One aggregated (gen, judge, condition[, criterion]) cell.

``pct`` treats ties as 0.5. ``wins`` / ``ties`` / ``losses`` are the raw
counts that feed it (``n = wins + ties + losses``). ``tie_frac`` is the
share of judgments that were explicit ties — so a 50% cell from all-ties
visually contrasts with a 50/50 split. ``ci_lo`` / ``ci_hi`` is the
Wilson 95% interval on ``pct`` (None when ``n == 0``).

ContextEvalArmOut.title: ContextEvalArmOut
ContextEvalArmOut.description: One arm (gold or candidate) of a context-builder eval.

ContextEvalDiffOut.title: ContextEvalDiffOut
ContextEvalDiffOut.description:
Side-by-side diff of two context-builder runs on the same question.

pages_only_in_gold/pages_only_in_candidate/pages_in_both are the result
of diffing the union of working_context, preloaded, and scope-linked
pages from each arm's context_built event. Headlines are carried via
PageRef so the UI can render links without an extra lookup.

ContextEvalExperimentOut.title: ContextEvalExperimentOut
ContextEvalExperimentOut.description:
One context-builder comparison (one gold arm paired with a candidate).

Surfaced in the experiments feed alongside ab_eval and run_call rows.
Links to the side-by-side diff page at
/context-evals/{gold_run_id}/vs/{candidate_run_id}.

CritiqueItem.title: CritiqueItem
CritiqueItem.description: One critic's free-form prose output for a given round.

DiagnosticsBundle.title: DiagnosticsBundle
DiagnosticsBundle.description:
Summary counts + three sections for the Diagnostics pane.

`biased_judge_count` uses a |A%-50| > 5pp threshold so the banner
line matches the default color thresholds in the UI.

EssayDetail.title: EssayDetail
EssayDetail.description: Essay + the prompts shown to completion / judge models.

EssayJudgmentsResponse.title: EssayJudgmentsResponse
EssayJudgmentsResponse.description:
Judgments for a single essay at one prefix variant.

Rows whose ``prefix_config_hash`` doesn't match the selected variant
are filtered out of ``judgments``. They split into two buckets:

- ``other_variant_hidden``: hash matches a *different* active prefix
  variant for this essay (e.g. you're viewing ``no_headers`` and 227
  rows belong to ``default``). Not stale — just a different cohort.
- ``stale_hidden``: hash matches no current variant. Genuinely stale
  from essay re-import drift or a prefix-config bump.

``orphaned`` flags a judgment whose source_a / source_b is not
present in versus_texts at the same prefix_hash -- the judgment
survived the variant check but its sources did not.

EssayMeta.title: EssayMeta
EssayMeta.description: Headline metadata for one cached essay.

EssayStatus.title: EssayStatus
EssayStatus.description:
Per-essay state for the /results "essays" panel.

Surfaces both validator output (clean? how many issues?) and the
current ``prefix_config_hash`` so a reader can spot when essay text
has drifted out from under cached judgments.

ForkOverrides.title: ForkOverrides
ForkOverrides.description:
Partial overrides for a base exchange.

Fields left as ``None`` inherit from the base. ``tools`` is a full
replacement — to remove a tool, omit it from the override; to add or
edit one, include the desired full Anthropic tool dict
(``{"name", "description", "input_schema"}``).

``thinking_off`` toggles adaptive thinking off for models that have it
enabled by default (Opus 4.7/4.6, Sonnet 4.6). Leaving as ``None``
inherits the model's default behavior; ``True`` disables thinking even
on models where it's normally always-on.

GenJudgeCell.title: GenJudgeCell
GenJudgeCell.description: One (gen_model, judge_model) cell with its rendered colors.

JobListItem.title: JobListItem
JobListItem.description: One row in GET /api/jobs. Built from a V1Job's metadata only.

JsonSchemaField.title: JsonSchemaField
JsonSchemaField.description: One field of a payload schema, projected for the atlas UI.

JudgeBiasRowOut.title: JudgeBiasRowOut
JudgeBiasRowOut.description:
Per-judge A-preference breakdown.

`all_*` covers every judged row; `cvc_*` covers only
completion-vs-completion pairs (neither side is human), which is the
pure-position-bias signal. `content_bias_pp` is the all - cvc gap in
percentage points; null when cvc n<20.

JudgeLabel.title: JudgeLabel
JudgeLabel.description: Stacked column-header parts for a judge model id.

Judgment.title: Judgment
Judgment.description:
One pairwise judgment row, shaped for the inspect view.

Includes the full ``reasoning_text`` / ``prompt`` / ``system_prompt``
so the inspect UI can render them inline (matching the standalone
self-vs-human HTML viewer). Older rows may not have ``system_prompt``
or ``prompt`` populated; both are optional.

JudgmentDetail.title: JudgmentDetail
JudgmentDetail.description:
Full judgment row for the side-panel inspector on /versus/results.

Includes the verbatim prompt + reasoning text + raw provider response,
so a reader can audit what the judge actually saw and said. Most fields
are optional because the shape varies across judge variants (OpenRouter
vs anthropic vs rumil:text vs rumil:orch; historical rumil:ws rows
also still appear).

JudgmentRow.title: JudgmentRow
JudgmentRow.description:
One row in the raw-judgments explorer at the bottom of /results.

``source_a``/``source_b`` are the alphabetical canonical ordering used
for the dedup key — stable identity across rows. ``display_first``/
``display_second`` are what the judge actually saw as "Continuation A"
and "B"; they diverge from canonical roughly half the time. The
``verdict`` letter (``A``/``B``/``tie``) refers to display order, not
canonical, so the rows-table renders display order alongside the
canonical pair to keep the mapping legible.

LiveRunSnapshot.title: LiveRunSnapshot
LiveRunSnapshot.description:
Snapshot of an in-flight (or recent) run for the live overlay UI.

A subset of WorkflowOverlay plus a few in-flight signals: the most
recent trace event timestamp, whether any call is still pending or
running, and a guessed "current stage" for highlighting.

MoveStats.title: MoveStats
MoveStats.description: Empirical stats for one MoveType across recent runs.

OrchestratorRunRequest.title: OrchestratorRunRequest
OrchestratorRunRequest.description:
Inputs for a remote orchestrator run.

Mirrors the subset of `main.py` flags that are forwarded into the
in-pod CLI invocation. Flags that only make sense on the laptop
(--db, --executor, --staged, --env-file, --run-id-file, --cli-user-id)
are intentionally absent — the in-pod run is always `--db prod
--executor local`.

Exactly one of `question` (start a new investigation) or
`continue_id` (resume an existing question by ID) must be set.

PathologyCounts.title: PathologyCounts
PathologyCounts.description:
Frequency of failure / instability patterns across a call type's
recent invocations. Each `*_pct` is a 0-100 fraction of the call
type's invocations exhibiting the pattern; `n_error_events` is a
count of trace ErrorEvents across all invocations.

A "lying COMPLETE" call is one with status=complete that nonetheless
emitted an ErrorEvent in its trace — the canonical silent-failure
pattern flagged by the open-issues mining pass.

PrefixVariantInfo.title: PrefixVariantInfo
PrefixVariantInfo.description:
One prefix variant available in the active config.

Surfaced on ResultsBundle so the /versus/results UI can render a
variant selector. ``id`` matches the yaml ``prefix.id`` /
``prefix_variants[].id`` and is the value the UI passes back as
``?prefix_label=<id>``.

PromptComposition.title: PromptComposition
PromptComposition.description: Ordered prompt parts that compose into a call's system prompt.

PromptDoc.title: PromptDoc
PromptDoc.description: A prompt markdown file rendered for the atlas UI.

PromptHistoryEntry.title: PromptHistoryEntry
PromptHistoryEntry.description:
One git commit that touched a prompt file.

``content_hash`` is sha-256 of the file as it stood at this commit
(not the git blob hash) — same shape as the live file's
``PromptDoc.content_hash`` so an iterator can spot-check whether a
particular run's prompt matched any of the historical revisions.

PromptPart.title: PromptPart
PromptPart.description: One file's contribution to a system-prompt composition.

PromptSection.title: PromptSection
PromptSection.description: One ## section within a prompt file.

ProvenanceAxis.title: ProvenanceAxis
ProvenanceAxis.description:
One axis of the provenance summary.

``description`` says what the value or hash is computed over, so
the operator knows what would change it. ``counts`` is value ->
row count over the surviving rows. ``current_values`` is the
mainline set (UI flags anything not in this list as non-current).
``value_labels`` turns opaque hashes into readable strings where
derivable (e.g. a ``prefix_config_hash`` maps back to its
originating ``"essay_id / variant_id"``).

ProvenanceSummary.title: ProvenanceSummary
ProvenanceSummary.description:
Per-axis breakdown of what slice the aggregate sits on top of.

``axes`` is keyed by axis name; ``axis_order`` declares the panel
rendering order (set by the backend's ``versus.mainline.axis_order``)
so the FE doesn't maintain a parallel list. New axes appear
automatically in the right place.

RecentBundle.title: RecentBundle
RecentBundle.description:
Recent-first feed for /versus/recent.

``texts`` and ``judgments`` are independently capped at ``limit`` so
the two tabs render without one starving the other. ``*_total`` is
the unfiltered table count for the header.

RecentTextRow.title: RecentTextRow
RecentTextRow.description:
One row in the chronological completions feed.

``flavor`` is a derived label so the FE doesn't have to re-parse
``source_id`` patterns: ``human`` / ``single-shot`` / ``orch`` /
``paraphrase`` / ``other``. ``workflow`` is populated for orch rows.
Optional generation knobs (``temperature``, ``max_tokens``,
``thinking_mode``, ``budget``, etc.) collapse the params snapshot to
just the bits worth showing in a compact table.

RedactedThinkingBlockOut.title: RedactedThinkingBlockOut
RedactedThinkingBlockOut.description: Anthropic-encrypted thinking block (content unavailable to clients).

RegistryRollup.title: RegistryRollup
RegistryRollup.description: Top-level /atlas/registry response — counts + summaries for the index.

RenderedPromptSample.title: RenderedPromptSample
RenderedPromptSample.description:
A real LLM exchange surfaced as a rendered-prompt sample.

The raw text of the system prompt + user messages as the model
actually saw them. Used to catch ``{{TASK}}`` leaks, parent-headline
pollution, and silent default mismatches that the
static composition view can't detect (since atlas's composition
only shows the template, not the substituted text).

``anomalies`` lists detected pattern matches the FE can highlight —
today: literal ``{{...}}`` token survivors, fallback-task placeholder
leak, missing preamble.

RowFilter.title: RowFilter
RowFilter.description:
Echoed back on ResultsBundle when the row list is filtered.

Populated from ``?filter_gen=`` / ``?filter_judge=`` /
``?filter_condition=`` / ``?filter_criterion=`` — the cell-drill-in
linkage from MatrixTable cells into the raw-judgments table below.

RunFlow.title: RunFlow
RunFlow.description:
Single run's flow — calls in order with which workflow stage they
correspond to (best-effort) and what they did.

RunOutcome.title: RunOutcome
RunOutcome.description:
Coarse "did this run succeed at its job" signal.

Pulled from runs.config.outcome when present (e.g. eval workflows
write a verdict there); else derived heuristically from run state —
completed-without-error vs aborted vs noop. UI can show as a small
badge per run.

RunRollup.title: RunRollup
RunRollup.description: Per-run summary used in workflow aggregate views.

Source.title: Source
Source.description:
One generated continuation (or the held-out human remainder).

``prompt`` is the verbatim completion prompt the model received,
stored on the row (legacy rows and the human baseline have no
prompt — the inspect UI falls back to the template in that case).

SourceMatrix.title: SourceMatrix
SourceMatrix.description:
A main matrix restricted to one essay source (e.g. forethought / redwood / carlsmith).

Wraps a regular :class:`Matrix` so the gen/judge axes match the all-source
matrix above; ``source_id`` is the essay-source label rendered in the UI.

ThinkingBlockOut.title: ThinkingBlockOut
ThinkingBlockOut.description: One summarized chain-of-thought block from an Anthropic response.

ThinkingBlocksOut.title: ThinkingBlocksOut
ThinkingBlocksOut.description:
Captured thinking content for an LLM exchange.

Populated when the model returned ``ThinkingBlock`` /
``RedactedThinkingBlock`` content. Null on the parent ``LLMExchangeOut``
when the model didn't think (e.g. Haiku) — clients should treat the
field as optional.

TraceEventRecord.title: TraceEventRecord
TraceEventRecord.description:
One trace event lifted from a call's ``trace_json``.

The ``payload`` dict holds the event's full body except the
discriminator ``event`` field (which lives on ``kind``); shape is
arbitrary so the FE can render typed events richly.

WorkflowStage.title: WorkflowStage
WorkflowStage.description: One stage in a workflow's spec — what runs, when, and with what.
